title: Season pages and expeditions phase
summary: Twenty-eighth structured packet for Eco Explorer. After the replay and season-wrap wave, the next larger step should not be squeezing a fourth route onto the current station board. Instead, the station should evolve into a notebook-like season-page pattern that can host one deeper expedition chapter, then use that pattern to launch a forest-first cave-and-climb exploration chapter built around the new `root-hollow` work.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the station pattern for season pages and one expedition slot.
execution_order.why_now: The current three-route board is full, so future growth needs a new pattern rather than more compression.

execution_order.goal: Add the first season-page station evolution and expedition slot.
execution_order.why_now: The game needs a growth-ready field-station structure before it opens a deeper chapter.

execution_order.goal: Review the station evolution for warmth, clarity, and handheld readability.
execution_order.why_now: A new station pattern only helps if it stays calm and notebook-like.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the first deeper expedition chapter around forest cave and climbable spaces.
execution_order.why_now: The newly expanded `root-hollow` and climbable forest work is the best current foundation for a more game-like exploration chapter.

execution_order.goal: Activate the first deeper expedition chapter.
execution_order.why_now: One stronger exploration-forward chapter is the next best evolution after the route replay wave.

execution_order.goal: Review the expedition chapter for difficulty, clarity, and whether it still fits the cozy tone.
execution_order.why_now: The exploration chapter should feel richer, not harsher.

execution_order.goal: Prepare expedition reward and support guidance.
execution_order.why_now: The expedition should get a small support hook only if it deepens the field-adventure loop.

execution_order.goal: Deepen the expedition with content and one tiny support hook if needed.
execution_order.why_now: The expedition should land as a real chapter, not just a shell.

execution_order.goal: Review the finished expedition chapter and decide whether the pattern is ready to carry future larger growth.
execution_order.why_now: This is the gate that decides whether expeditions become a durable second structure in the game.

execution_order.goal: Add one tiny second-expedition teaser if the first expedition pattern proves durable.
execution_order.why_now: The station pattern should show it can grow further without immediately launching another full expedition.

guardrails: Do not compress a fourth full route onto the existing three-route board.
Keep the station notebook-like instead of turning it into a multi-tab dashboard.
Keep the expedition calm, readable, and kid-friendly.
Do not add combat, fail states, or a survival loop.
Do not broaden the game into a heavy inventory or loot system.
acceptance_focus: The field station can grow beyond three routes without losing its calm handheld feel.
The first expedition chapter makes exploration feel more game-like without changing genre.
The expedition uses the existing cave and climbable work as a coherent chapter rather than as isolated niches.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, docs/reports/2026-03-30-season-pages-and-expeditions-phase.md, and this packet. Treat the replay and season-wrap wave as the last dense three-route phase. The next larger move is to evolve the station into season pages plus one expedition slot, then launch a deeper forest-first expedition chapter built around the live cave and climbable exploration work.
